columns.description: Lowercase SHA-256 from stable_digest of the exact-key payload {asset_id, issuer_id, namespace: ame_stocks.identity.ticker_alias, rule_version: ame_stocks_ticker_alias_id_from_observed_interval_v1, share_class_id, ticker, valid_from_session}; nullable hierarchy keys are explicit JSON null, dates are YYYY-MM-DD strings, and no key may be omitted or added.
columns.name: ticker_alias_id

columns.description: Fixed ame_stocks_ticker_alias_id_from_observed_interval_v1 rule using json.dumps with allow_nan=false, ensure_ascii=true, separators comma/colon, sort_keys=true; UTF-8, no newline, and SHA-256 lowercase hex.
columns.name: ticker_alias_id_rule_version

columns.description: Foreign key to identity/asset_master; never derived from ticker or CIK alone.
columns.name: asset_id

columns.description: Exact case-sensitive provider ticker preserved without trimming, casefolding, root parsing, or punctuation normalization.
columns.name: ticker

columns.description: First XNYS session in the observed active interval, inclusive.
columns.name: valid_from_session

columns.description: Last XNYS session in the observed active interval, inclusive.
columns.name: valid_through_session

columns.description: Next ordered distinct XNYS source session in the fixed S4 release after valid_through_session; null only when right-censored at the fixed S4 coverage end. It is calendar/session-spine derived even when the ticker is absent on that next session.
columns.name: valid_to_session_exclusive

columns.description: Exact domain membership_absent_or_identity_changed_next_source_session or right_censored_at_source_end.
columns.name: interval_end_status

columns.description: Count of XNYS membership sessions in this maximal interval.
columns.name: interval_session_count

columns.description: Exact Composite FIGI constant within the interval and reproducing asset_id.
columns.name: composite_figi

columns.description: Deterministic share-class parent identity when an exact share-class FIGI is available for the interval.
columns.name: share_class_id

columns.description: Exact share-class FIGI corresponding to share_class_id; multiple Composite-FIGI assets may share it.
columns.name: share_class_figi

columns.description: CIK-derived issuer reference constant within the interval; it is not an asset merge key.
columns.name: issuer_id

columns.description: Exact S7 method, led by source_composite_figi_exact; Share-class FIGI and CIK remain corroborating hierarchy evidence, while uniquely associated S5 events only corroborate an occurrence inside an already observed interval and never a boundary.
columns.name: alias_resolution_method

columns.description: Exact domain resolved_strong or resolved_temporal; weak ticker-date candidates are excluded from this table.
columns.name: alias_resolution_status

columns.description: Whether at least one accepted S5 event occurrence is uniquely associated to this interval by exact Composite FIGI, exact case-sensitive ticker, ordinary_calendar_date quality, and calendar-date containment within the observed inclusive interval; false does not invalidate observed S4 membership.
columns.name: ticker_event_corroborated

columns.description: Count of distinct accepted S5 ticker_change_event source_record_id values whose response_composite_figi and effective_ticker exactly equal this interval, whose event_date_quality is ordinary_calendar_date, and whose event_date lies between valid_from_session and valid_through_session inclusive. The event must match exactly one emitted interval; all other events count only in build-level association review and never define a prior ticker or interval boundary.
columns.name: ticker_event_count

columns.description: Number of S4 active universe rows collapsed into the interval.
columns.name: source_row_count

columns.description: S4 selected_source_record_id for valid_from_session.
columns.name: first_source_record_id

columns.description: S4 selected_source_record_id for valid_through_session.
columns.name: last_source_record_id

columns.description: True only when every session in the interval has a strong, non-conflicted asset resolution.
columns.name: backtest_identity_eligible

columns.description: Fixed reconstructed_historical_active_membership_v1 marker inherited from S4 session evidence.
columns.name: membership_time_scope

columns.description: Fixed metadata_as_returned_at_source_capture_not_historical_vintage_v1 marker.
columns.name: metadata_time_scope

columns.description: Maximum source_available_session over the transitive accepted-source evidence closure used for the interval, its asset/share/issuer hierarchy, eligibility, counts, corroboration, and conflict disposition; S5 request-status-only coverage is excluded.
columns.name: identity_evidence_available_session

columns.description: Fixed s7_ticker_interval_resolution_v1 interval segmentation, evidence precedence, and event-corroboration rule version.
columns.name: resolution_rule_version

columns.description: Exact protected S4 release-set ID.
columns.name: source_s4_release_set_id

columns.description: Exact S5 ticker-event request-status release ID used only for coverage and 404 semantics.
columns.name: source_s5_status_release_id

columns.description: Exact S5 ticker-change-event release ID.
columns.name: source_s5_event_release_id

columns.description: Exact S6 ticker-overview-safe release ID.
columns.name: source_s6_overview_release_id

description: XNYS-session ticker intervals derived from observed S4 active membership after Composite-FIGI asset resolution. S5 event dates corroborate boundaries but never create predecessor tickers or intervals by themselves.
grain: One maximal XNYS-consecutive interval for the same asset_id, exact ticker, share_class_id, and issuer_id; a gap or one of those identity-field changes starts a new interval, while resolution status alone is a versioned attribute and does not change ticker_alias_id.

qa_rules.description: Every output field, order, Arrow type, and nullability must equal this contract.
Every fixed S4 release-set member and fixed S5/S6 release must pass release-manifest, build-manifest, schema, checksum, row-count, and workflow trust-chain verification before use.

Every row and each coordinated member build must bind the exact approved S4 three-release set, S5 request-status release, S5 event release, and S6 overview release; all four builds must share one exact six-dataset source digest and latest-by-time discovery is forbidden.
ticker_alias_id must be non-null and unique.
Every asset_id must resolve to a v1 active_identity asset_master row.
Every non-null issuer_id must resolve to issuer_master.
Every ticker_alias_id must reproduce from the exact compact sorted-JSON payload, explicit null encoding, exact ticker/date encoding, and approved fixed vectors.

ticker_alias_id and all identity FKs must be lowercase 64-hex values generated under the exact approved asset, share-class, issuer, and alias rule versions; ticker/date/null normalization must match the frozen payload contract.

interval_end_status must be membership_absent_or_identity_changed_next_source_session or right_censored_at_source_end; alias_resolution_method must be source_composite_figi_exact or source_composite_figi_exact_temporal_hierarchy; alias_resolution_status must be resolved_strong or resolved_temporal; scope and rule fields must equal reconstructed_historical_active_membership_v1, metadata_as_returned_at_source_capture_not_historical_vintage_v1, ame_stocks_ticker_alias_id_from_observed_interval_v1, and s7_ticker_interval_resolution_v1.

source_composite_figi_exact pairs only with resolved_strong; source_composite_figi_exact_temporal_hierarchy pairs only with resolved_temporal. Both require asset/composite, optional jointly-null-or-valid share pair, optional valid issuer, and eligibility=true. ticker_event_corroborated equals ticker_event_count>0; right-censored status iff exclusive end is null, otherwise membership-absent-or-changed status with the next source-session endpoint.
interval_session_count, ticker_event_count, and source_row_count must be non-negative native int64 values, and an emitted interval must have positive session and source counts.

ticker_event_count must equal the number of distinct accepted S5 ticker_change_event source_record_id values with exact response_composite_figi=interval composite_figi, exact effective_ticker=interval ticker, event_date_quality=ordinary_calendar_date, and valid_from_session<=event_date<=valid_through_session, provided the event matches exactly one emitted interval. Source_row_count and interval_session_count must exactly recompute from matching S4 daily membership rows. Sentinel/cluster-quality, pair-mismatch, out-of-interval, zero-match, multi-match, and S5 request-status rows are excluded from interval counts; no count is accepted from a prior aggregate.

identity_evidence_available_session must equal max(source_available_session) over the transitive accepted-source evidence closure used for this interval, its referenced asset/share/issuer state, eligibility, counts, hierarchy, corroboration, or conflict disposition. The closure includes every participating S4 selected row across sessions/entities, accepted S6 Overview rows, and only accepted S5 ticker_change_event rows uniquely associated by the exact event rule; unassociated or ambiguous S5 events, S5 request status, and sibling output rows are excluded, and every alias has non-empty S4 evidence.
valid_from_session must not exceed valid_through_session, and a non-null exclusive end must follow valid_through_session.
interval_session_count must equal the XNYS sessions in the inclusive observed interval.
source_row_count must equal interval_session_count because the active S4 source has one exact ticker row per included source session.
Adjacent intervals with identical identity fields and no XNYS gap must have been merged.
No interval may bridge an inactive or absent S4 membership session.
One exact ticker on one session may not resolve to more than one asset.
No two intervals for the same exact ticker may overlap on the fixed S4 source-session spine, even when they reference the same asset.
first_source_record_id and last_source_record_id must match the exact S4 rows on valid_from_session and valid_through_session respectively.

In the coordinated group build, each eligible universe_daily row must be covered by exactly one matching alias and every expanded alias session must map back to exactly one active S4/universe row with the same ticker, asset, and Composite FIGI.
Every interval asset must equal the asset generated from its exact Composite FIGI.
Non-null share_class_id and share_class_figi must reproduce each other and may parent multiple assets.
S5 request-status rows, including HTTP 404, may measure endpoint coverage only and may never create, delete, truncate, or extend an alias interval.

S5 events may corroborate only after exact Composite/ticker, ordinary date quality, and unique interval containment association; they may never create, extend, split, truncate, coalesce, or be the sole source of an interval or predecessor ticker.

No accepted S5 ticker_change_event source_record_id may associate to more than one emitted alias interval under the exact Composite/ticker, ordinary date quality, and inclusive calendar-date containment rule; ties fail the build rather than being broken by order, proximity, or source ordinal.

The four sibling outputs must share one ephemeral resolution graph, identical six-release inputs/source digest/binding ID, and passing cross-table QA; sibling staging or unpublished outputs may not be source artifacts.
Only intervals reaching the fixed S4 coverage end may have a null exclusive end.
Every emitted ticker_alias row must have backtest_identity_eligible=true and cover only strong non-conflicted daily resolutions; weak or conflicted intervals are excluded from this table.
Observed same-ticker incompatible Composite evidence remains a High issue and suppresses alias admission; S6 lifecycle envelopes alone do not prove overlap.
Share-class-, CIK-, or ticker-date-only candidate mappings remain outside ticker_alias until separately approved.

Build-level count of accepted S5 ticker_change_event rows excluded from interval corroboration because date quality is not ordinary_calendar_date, the exact Composite/ticker pair is absent, the calendar date is outside every matching observed interval, or the event does not match exactly one interval. Report reason counts and bounded examples; these rows remain upstream event evidence and never alter interval membership.
